IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: PROMOTE BETTERMENT OF MEDICAL & DENTAL HEALTH OF THE PUBLIC FOR THE GENERAL WELFARE OF THE COMMUNITY
IRS990.Desc: HAWAII DENTAL SERVICE (HDS) OFFERS AND ADMINISTERS DENTAL CARE PROGRAMS TO ABOUT 644,000 INDIVIDUALS THROUGHOUT THE STATE OF HAWAII. AS A MEMBER OF THE DELTA DENTAL PLANS ASSOCIATION, A NATIONAL ASSOCIATION SUPPORTING DELTAS NATION-WIDE, HDS SUBSCRIBERS ARE ABLE TO ACCESS DELTA CONTRACTED PROVIDERS IN EVERY STATE. IN 2024, HDS PROCESSED OVER 1.2 MILLION CLAIMS FOR SUBSCRIBERS AND THEIR DEPENDENTS. TO ENSURE PROVIDERS ARE SUBMITTING CLAIMS APPROPRIATELY, HDS CONTINUALLY AUDITS CLAIMS. IN ADDITION, HDS AWARDED OVER $1.9 MILLION IN GRANTS TO OTHER CHARITABLE ORGANIZATIONS.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: MANAGING THE DENTAL BENEFITS OF ELIGIBLE BENEFICIARIES COVERED BY THE STATE OF HAWAII MED-QUEST PROGRAM.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: PROGRAM-RELATED INVESTMENTS. HDS INVESTS IN CERTAIN PROGRAM-RELATED INVESTMENTS WITH OTHER DELTA DENTAL COMPANIES TO PROVIDE DENTAL CARE TO INDIVIDUALS, GROUPS AND MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC AND GENERALLY TO DO ALL THINGS NECESSARY TO RENDER DENTAL SERVICES MORE AVAILABLE. FMG HAS CONTRACTS WITH COMMUNITY CARE NETWORK (CCN), FEDERAL EMPLOYEES DENTAL AND VISION INSURANCE (FEDVIP), AND VETERAN AFFAIRS DENTAL INSURANCE PROGRAM (VADIP).

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION IS INCLUDED IN THE CONSOLIDATED AUDITED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS OF HAWAII DENTAL SERVICE, HAWAII DENTAL SERVICE FOUNDATION, AND HAWAII CLIENT SERVICES CORPORATION. THE HDS AUDIT COMMITTEE, WHICH CONSISTS OF BOARD MEMBERS, SELECTS THE AUDITORS AND REVIEWS THE CONSOLIDATED AUDITED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE D, PART XI, LINE 2D - OTHER ADJUSTMENTS
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: EQUITY INVESTMENT IN CORPORATE SUBSIDIARY - $177,343 GRANT TO HDSF - $1,500,000

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE D, PART XI, LINE 4B - OTHER ADJUSTMENTS
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: IN ACCORDANCE WITH GAAP, AUDITED FINANCIALS DO NOT INCLUDE SELF-INSURED PREMIUMS AND RELATED CLAIM PAYMENTS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE D, PART XII, LINE 4B - OTHER ADJUSTMENTS
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: IN ACCORDANCE WITH GAAP, AUDITED FINANCIALS DO NOT INCLUDE SELF-INSURED PREMIUMS AND RELATED CLAIM PAYMENTS.

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: SUPPORT FOR COMMUNITY SERVICES PROVIDED BY THE NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION
SUPPORT FOR COMMUNITY SERVICES PROVIDED BY THE NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION & WALK TO END ALZHEIMER'S SPONSORSHIP
SUPPORT FOR COMMUNITY SERVICES PROVIDED BY THE NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION & HEART WALK SPONSORSHIP
OUTSTANDING MOTHER AWARDS AND PROGRAM SUPPORT

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: BOYS AND GIRLS CLUB OF HAWAII

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: SUPPORT FOR COMMUNITY SERVICES PROVIDED BY THE NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION & 28TH ANNUAL WALK IN THE COUNTRY DINNER SPONSORSHIP

2024-2025 GIRLS FLAG FOOTBALL SPONSORSHIP
SUPPORT FOR COMMUNITY SERVICES PROVIDED BY THE NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION & 50TH ANNIVERSARY PAINA SPONSORSHIP

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: MARCH OF DIMES (MARCH FOR BABIES OAHU)

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: SUPPORT FOR COMMUNITY SERVICES PROVIDED BY THE NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION & SIGNATURE CHEFS FEEDING MOTHERHOOD SPONSORSHIP
SUPPORT FOR COMMUNITY SERVICES PROVIDED BY THE NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION AND ULU HANA PEWA AWARDS - KOKUA SPONSOR

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: RONALD MCDONALD HOUSE CHARITIES OF HAWAII

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: SUPPORT FOR COMMUNITY SERVICES PROVIDED BY THE NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION & HOPE AND HEROES 2024 GALA SPONSORSHIP
SUPPORT FOR COMMUNITY SERVICES PROVIDED BY THE NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION
SUPPORT FOR COMMUNITY SERVICES FOR MAUI RELIEF EFFORTS PROVIDED BY THE NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION AND BRING JOY HOLIDAY SPONSORSHIP
SUPPORT FOR COMMUNITY SERVICES PROVIDED BY THE NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION AND VARIOUS SPONSORSHIPS

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE PURPOSE OF THE GRANTS AWARDED BY HAWAII DENTAL SERVICE (HDS) ARE REVIEWED AND APPROVED BY THE HDS CEO AND ANOTHER EXECUTIVE. OTHER DONATIONS ARE CONTRIBUTED TO VARIOUS PUBLIC CHARITABLE ORGANIZATIONS TO SUPPORT THE EXEMPT FUNCTIONS OF THE ORGANIZATIONS AND ARE NOT MONITORED AFTER AWARDED. HOWEVER, IF THE CEO AND/OR EXECUTIVE HAVE A POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST, HE OR SHE WILL RECUSE THEMSELVES FROM THE SELECTION PROCESS, AND/OR BOARD OF DIRECTOR WILL MAKE THE DECISION ON THE GRANTS.

SUPPLEMENTAL COMPENSATION INFORMATION: HDS HOLDS A CORPORATE MEMBERSHIP IN A SOCIAL CLUB IN WHICH THE CLUB REQUIREMENTS DICTATE THAT AN INDIVIDUAL BE IDENTIFIED TO AUTHORIZE TRANSACTIONS. AS SUCH, HDS'S CEO HAS BEEN DESIGNATED AS THE AUTHORIZED INDIVIDUAL. ONLY BUSINESS TRANSACTIONS ARE ALLOWED TO BE PAID BY HDS AND MUST BE SUBSTANTIATED BY PROPER DOCUMENTATION. DISCRETIONARY SPENDING ACCOUNT: ONLY BUSINESS RELATED TRANSACTIONS ARE ALLOWED AND SUBSTANTIATED BY PROPER DOCUMENTATION.
FIRST CLASS TRAVEL IS AVAILABLE TO THE PRESIDENT/CEO AND BOARD CHAIR PERSON FOR TRAVEL OF 3,000 MILES OR MORE.

NON-FIXED PAYMENTS: HDS HAS AN INCENTIVE PLAN DETAILING THE INCENTIVE CALCULATION WHEN THE COMPANY ACHIEVES CERTAIN PERFORMANCE STANDARDS AND STRATEGIC GOALS. THE INCENTIVE AMOUNT VARIES EACH YEAR DEPENDING ON ACHIEVEMENT OF STRATEGIC GOALS. INCENTIVE AMOUNTS FOR OFFICERS ARE APPROVED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS COMPENSATION COMMITTEE WHICH IS DOCUMENTED IN MINUTES. INCENTIVE AMOUNTS FOR MANAGEMENT AND STAFF ARE APPROVED BY OFFICERS AND THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS THROUGH THE ANNUAL BUDGETING PROCESS.

NOTE 1 - PREMIUMS FOR DENTAL PLAN - EMPLOYER GROUP PURCHASES AN HDS DENTAL PLAN BUT THE DIRECTOR DOES NOT INFLUENCE DECISION MAKING IN PURCHASING THE DENTAL PLAN FROM HDS.NOTE 2 - DENTIST PARTICIPATING IN HDS PROVIDER NETWORK OFFERING DENTAL SERVICES TO HDS SUBSCRIBERS.NOTE 3 -PREMIUMS ARE RECEIVED FROM VARIOUS ORGANIZATIONS IN WHICH OFFICERS AND DIRECTORS OF HDS SERVE AS BOARD MEMBERS.

SMILEFUND GRANT FROM HAWAII DENTAL SERVICE FOUNDATION (HDSF) TO THE UNIVERSITY OF HAWAII FOUNDATION (UHF). THIS GRANT FROM HDSF TO UHF WAS APPROVED TO PAY FOR THE REGISTRATION FEES FOR DR. MATTHEW OISHI TO ATTEND THE PACIFIC REGION INDIGENOUS DOCTORS CONGRESS AND PRESENT HIS ORAL HEALTH RESEARCH FINDINGS TO MEDICAL COLLEAGUES. THE GRANT WAS AWARDED ON AN OBJECTIVE, NONDISCRIMINARY BASIS BASED ON PRE-ESTABLISHED CRITERIA. DR. MATTHEW OISHI IS THE ORAL HEALTH DIRECTOR AT THE JOHN A. BURNS SCHOOL OF MEDICINE AT THE UNIVERSITY OF HAWAII AND IS THE SON OF DR. MARTIN OISHI WHO IS A HAWAII DENTAL SERVICE (HDS) BOARD MEMBER. HDSF IS A CONTROLLED ENTITY OF HDS (EIN: 99-0107971). DR. MARTIN OISHI IS NOT ON THE BOARD OF HDSF, NOR WAS THE GRANT DIRECTLY TO DR. MATTHEW OISHI. THE GRANT WAS AWARDED AT THE DISCRETION OF THE HDSF DIRECTOR, AND DUE TO THE SIZE OF THE GRANT, THE GRANT DID NOT REQUIRE BOARD APPROVAL.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 6
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: DESCRIPTION OF ORGANIZATION'S MEMBERS: MEMBERSHIP IS SOLELY AVAILABLE TO DENTISTS WHO HAVE BEEN DULY LICENSED TO PRACTICE IN THE STATE OF HAWAII AND WHO MEET ANY ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS CONSISTENT WITH APPLICABLE LAW ESTABLISHED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS FROM TIME TO TIME. APPLICATION IS MADE IN WRITING AND APPROVED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS. MEMBERS MAY AT ANY TIME BY A MEETING DULY CALLED FOR SUCH PURPOSE DEPOSE OR REMOVE FROM OFFICE ANY DENTIST DIRECTOR, WITH OR WITHOUT CAUSE, EXCEPT AS SUCH REMOVAL WOULD BE CONTRARY TO LAW.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ELECT BOARD MEMBERS A MAJORITY OF THE MEMBERS OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS SHALL BE COMPRISED OF NON-DENTIST REPRESENTATIVES FROM THE GENERAL PUBLIC AND THE REMAINING MEMBERS SHALL BE MEMBER DENTISTS. MEMBER DENTISTS AND PUBLIC DIRECTORS RESPECTIVELY ELECT DENTISTS AND PUBLIC DIRECTORS TO THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS FOR A THREE YEAR TERM.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: APPROVAL OF DECISIONS: BYLAWS AND ARTICLES OF INCORPORATION MAY BE ALTERED OR REPEALED, OR NEW BYLAWS AND ARTICLES OF INCORPORATION MAY BE ADOPTED IN LIEU THEREOF, BY ADOPTION OF A RESOLUTION BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS SETTING FORTH THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT AND DIRECTING THAT IT BE SUBMITTED TO A VOTE AT THE ANNUAL OR SPECIAL MEETING OF THE MEMBERS. THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT SHALL BE ADOPTED UPON RECEIVING AT LEAST TWO-THIRDS OF THE VOTES WHICH MEMBERS PRESENT IN PERSON AT THE MEETING ARE ENTITLED TO CAST. THE BYLAWS MAY ALSO BE AMENDED BY AFFIRMATIVE VOTE OF THE DIRECTORS THEN IN OFFICE, WITHOUT FURTHER ACTION BY THE MEMBERS, FOR THE PURPOSE OF CURING AMBIGUITIES, CORRECTING OR SUPPLEMENTING DEFECTIVE OR INCONSISTENT PROVISIONS, OR ENABLING THE CORPORATION TO COMPLY WITH APPLICABLE LAWS AND REGULATIONS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: APPROVED COPY OF FORM 990: THE RETURN IS THOROUGHLY REVIEWED BY THE CONTROLLER PRIOR TO FINAL REVIEW WITH THE CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER AND PRESIDENT. A COPY OF FORM 990 IS PROVIDED TO THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS PRIOR TO FILING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: WRITTEN CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY: THE HDS COMPLIANCE PROGRAM CONTAINS THE HDS CONFLICTS OF INTEREST POLICY. ALL EMPLOYEES, BOARD OF DIRECTORS, AND OFFICERS OF HDS ARE REQUIRED TO COMPLETE THE CONFLICTS OF INTEREST DISCLOSURE STATEMENT AND CERTIFICATION TO DISCLOSE ANY ARRANGEMENTS WHICH MAY CONSTITUTE AN ACTUAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST OR GIVE RISE TO THE APPEARANCE OF A CONFLICT OF INTEREST ANNUALLY. THE HDS COMPLIANCE DEPARTMENT REVIEWS THE ANNUAL CONFLICTS OF INTEREST DISCLOSURE STATEMENT AND CERTIFICATIONS AND REPORTS POTENTIAL CONFLICTS OF INTERESTS TO THE HDS BOARD OF DIRECTORS. ANY ACTUAL OR POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST MAY ALSO BE RAISED BY ANY OTHER MEMBER OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OR THROUGH NORMAL REPORTING PROCESSES AT HDS. IN ADDITION, THE ORGANIZATION PROVIDES ANNUAL FIDUCIARY TRAINING TO ITS DIRECTORS AND OFFICERS AND REVIEWS SCENARIOS WHICH MAY GIVE RISE TO A CONFLICT OF INTEREST. AN INTERESTED PERSON MAY MAKE A PRESENTATION TO THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS, BUT AFTER SUCH PRESENTATION, THE INTERESTED PERSON SHALL RECUSE THEMSELVES FROM THE MEETING DURING THE DISCUSSION OF, AND THE VOTE ON, THE ARRANGEMENT INVOLVING THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST OR THE APPEARANCE OF A CONFLICT OF INTEREST.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: PROCESS FOR DETERMINING COMPENSATION: COMPENSATION FOR THE CEO AND OFFICERS ARE BASED ON ADVICE FROM AN EXTERNAL COMPENSATION CONSULTANT USING RELEVANT MARKET DATA FOR COMPARABLE POSITIONS. OFFICERS COMPENSATION IS APPROVED BY THE BOARD'S COMPENSATION COMMITTEE AND DOCUMENTED IN MINUTES. COMPENSATION ADJUSTMENTS ARE PROCESSED WITH THE COMPANY WIDE INCREASES ANNUALLY. COMPENSATION FOR NON-OFFICER ROLES ARE ADJUSTED BASED ON RELEVANT MARKET DATA AND APPROVED BY THE OFFICERS OF HDS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: TAX RETURNS AND OTHER REQUIRED DOCUMENTS ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST.
CHANGE IN ACCRUED POSTRETIREMENT LIABILITIES 4,957,460. ROUNDING 3.
